input.prompt:
My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere.
Can you generate a file system wrapper for me?

data.content: Your codebase uses `IFileSystem` directly. I'll generate a facade wrapper that centralizes common file operations with error handling, making it easy to mock and test.
data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple codebase using `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`. They want a file system wrapper. But what kind of wrapper? 

Looking at this, they already use `IFileSystem` which is an abstraction. A "file system wrapper" typically means a concrete implementation that wraps the real file system, or perhaps they want their own abstraction layer on top of `IFileSystem`.

Actually, re-reading the request: "My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere. Can you generate a file system wrapper for me?"

This likely means they want a wrapper class that implements `IFileSystem` (or wraps it) - perhaps a custom implementation that delegates to the real `FileSystem` class from `System.IO.Abstractions`, or perhaps a simplified wrapper interface of their own.
 
Since `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the concrete implementation of `IFileSystem`, a wrapper would most likely add value through logging, error handling, or other cross-cutting concerns - or they might want a simplified interface that only exposes the methods they actually use.

The most practical approach would be creating a `FileSystemWrapper` class that acts as a facade over `IFileSystem`, giving them their own abstraction layer to own rather than depending directly on the external library.

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-62183052b6b54899bb287902017c438f/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs b/tmp/sv-62183052b6b54899bb287902017c438f/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-62183052b6b54899bb287902017c438f/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,89 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Delegates all operations to <see cref="IFileSystem"/> from System.IO.Abstractions.
+/// Register as a singleton in DI alongside <see cref="IFileSystem"/>.
+/// </summary>
+public class FileSystemWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemWrapper
+{
+    // File operations
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, IEnumerable<string> lines) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, lines);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream OpenWrite(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Open(path, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.Write);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    // Directory operations
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.EnumerateFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> EnumerateDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.EnumerateDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // Path operations
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string? GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileNameWithoutExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
+
+    public string? GetExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+
